---
title: "PlotDataExport Library"
description: "Qt-free CSV serialization for raster, PSTH, and heatmap plot data"
---

## Overview

`PlotDataExport` is a header-only, Qt-free library in namespace `PlotDataExport` that serializes plot data to CSV strings. Free functions take core data types (`GatherResult`, `HistogramData`, `HeatmapRowData`, `PerTrialHeatmapInput`) and return CSV suitable for analysis in Python (pandas/seaborn/matplotlib) and R (readr/ggplot2).

The library handles **serialization only**. File dialogs, button placement, and user interaction are the responsibility of each plot widget.

## Location

`src/PlotDataExport/`

## Dependencies

```
PlotDataExport (INTERFACE)
├── GatherResult
├── CorePlotting (HistogramData, HeatmapRowData)
└── TimeFrame
```

No Qt dependency. No IO library dependency.

## Headers

| Header | Primary export function |
|--------|-------------------------|
| `RasterCSVExport.hpp` | `exportRasterToCSV()` |
| `HistogramCSVExport.hpp` | `exportHistogramToCSV()` |
| `HeatmapCSVExport.hpp` | `exportHeatmapToCSV()`, `exportHeatmapPerTrialToCSV()` |

All functions accept an optional `std::string_view delimiter` (default `","`).

### RasterCSVExport.hpp

Exports raster/event plot data to **long-form tidy CSV**.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `trial_index` | int | Trial number (0-based, respects gather order) |
| `event_key` | string | Name of the event series |
| `relative_time` | double | Time relative to alignment event (in time units) |

**Input struct** — `RasterSeriesInput`:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `event_key` | `std::string` | Series name written to the `event_key` column |
| `gathered` | `GatherResult<DigitalEventSeries> const *` | Trial-aligned views (non-null) |
| `time_frame` | `TimeFrame const *` | Converts `TimeFrameIndex` to absolute time (non-null) |

Relative time per event:

`time_frame->getTimeAtIndex(event.time()) - gathered->alignmentTimeAt(trial_index)`

**Metadata** — `RasterExportMetadata`: `alignment_key`, `window_size`.

Comment header `export_type`: `raster`.

### HistogramCSVExport.hpp

Exports PSTH/histogram data to **long-form tidy CSV**.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `bin_center` | double | Center of the time bin |
| `event_key` | string | Name of the event series |
| `value` | double | Bin value (count, firing rate, z-score — depends on scaling) |

**Input struct** — `HistogramSeriesInput`:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `event_key` | `std::string` | Series name |
| `data` | `CorePlotting::HistogramData const *` | Pre-computed histogram (non-null) |

**Metadata** — `HistogramExportMetadata`: `alignment_key`, `window_size`, `scaling_mode`, `estimation_method`.

Comment header `export_type`: `psth`.

### HeatmapCSVExport.hpp

Two export modes:

**Aggregate** — `exportHeatmapToCSV()` produces a **wide matrix CSV**.

- Header row: `unit_key, <bin_center_0>, <bin_center_1>, ...`
- Data rows: one per unit; bin centers are reconstructed from the first row's `bin_start` and `bin_width`.
- Input: parallel `std::vector<std::string>` unit keys and `std::vector<CorePlotting::HeatmapRowData>` rows (same length).

Comment header `export_type`: `heatmap_aggregate`.

**Per-trial** — `exportHeatmapPerTrialToCSV()` produces **long-form tidy CSV**.

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `unit_key` | string | Name of the neural unit |
| `trial_index` | int | Trial number (0-based) |
| `bin_center` | double | Center of the time bin |
| `value` | double | Per-trial bin value |

**Input struct** — `PerTrialHeatmapInput`:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `unit_key` | `std::string` | Unit name |
| `times` | `std::vector<double>` | Bin centers (shared across trials) |
| `per_trial_values` | `std::vector<std::vector<double>>` | `[trial_idx][time_idx]` |

`PerTrialHeatmapInput` is self-contained so the library does not depend on `EventRateEstimation` (UI layer). Widgets convert `RateEstimateWithTrials` to `PerTrialHeatmapInput` before calling the export API.

Comment header `export_type`: `heatmap_per_trial`.

**Metadata** — `HeatmapExportMetadata`: `alignment_key`, `window_size`, `scaling_mode`, `estimation_method`.

## Metadata Comment Header

All export functions prepend a comment block (lines starting with `#`). Optional metadata fields are omitted when empty; `window_size` is written only when `> 0.0`.

Example (raster):

```
# export_type: raster
# alignment_key: stimulus_onset
# window_size: 1000.0
trial_index,event_key,relative_time
0,spikes,-245.3
0,spikes,-102.1
...
```

Both Python (`pd.read_csv(comment='#')`) and R (`read_csv(comment='#')`) skip these lines natively.

## Widget Integration Pattern

Each plot widget follows the same pattern:

1. **PropertiesWidget** adds an "Export CSV" button and emits an `exportCSVRequested()` signal.
2. **WidgetRegistration** connects the signal to `handleExportCSV()` on the main widget.
3. **Main widget** opens `AppFileDialog`, collects data from the rendering widget, calls the appropriate `PlotDataExport` function, and writes the CSV string to disk.

```
PropertiesWidget  ──signal──▶  MainWidget::handleExportCSV()
                                   │
                                   ├─ AppFileDialog::getSaveFileName()
                                   ├─ collect*ExportData() on OpenGL widget
                                   ├─ PlotDataExport::export*ToCSV()
                                   └─ write string to file
```

## Consuming Widgets

| Widget | Export function | Data collection |
|--------|----------------|-----------------|
| EventPlotWidget | `exportRasterToCSV()` | `EventPlotOpenGLWidget::collectRasterExportData()` |
| PSTHWidget | `exportHistogramToCSV()` | `PSTHPlotOpenGLWidget::collectHistogramExportData()` |
| HeatmapWidget | `exportHeatmapToCSV()` (aggregate only) | `HeatmapOpenGLWidget::collectHeatmapExportData()` |

`exportHeatmapPerTrialToCSV()` is implemented and tested in the library but **not yet wired** in `HeatmapWidget` — the widget always exports the aggregate wide-matrix format.

Widget CMake targets link `PlotDataExport` privately: `EventPlotWidget`, `PSTHWidget`, `HeatmapWidget`.

## Testing

Unit tests live in `tests/PlotDataExport/` (`test_RasterCSVExport.cpp`, `test_HistogramCSVExport.cpp`, `test_HeatmapCSVExport.cpp`). Tests construct small data objects with known values and verify CSV output (columns, values, row counts, delimiters, metadata comments). No Qt or OpenGL fixtures are required.

Because `PlotDataExport` depends on `GatherResult` → `TransformsV2`, the test executable uses `--whole-archive` linking for `TransformsV2` (same pattern as other transform-dependent tests).

```bash
ctest --preset linux-clang-release --output-on-failure -R "PlotDataExport"
```

## See Also

- [User Guide: CSV Plot Export](../../user_guide/export/csv_plot_export.qmd) — end-user documentation with Python and R loading examples
- [GatherResult](../GatherResult/index.qmd) — trial-aligned views used by raster export
- [CorePlotting](../CorePlotting/index.qmd) — `HistogramData` and `HeatmapRowData`
- [EventRateEstimation](../Plots/Common/EventRateEstimation.qmd) — `RateEstimateWithTrials` (widgets convert this to `PerTrialHeatmapInput` for per-trial export)
